notes: Kalliope DE-611-BF-115088. Teilnachlass 1801-1850, 2 linear meters. Copies/photocopies of Part 1 plus private correspondence incl. family letters.

Kalliope DE-611-BF-14941. Nachlass: ~6000 letters, Tellow accounting records, manuscripts, genealogical materials, personal library, photographs. Published guide: Veroeffentlichungen der UB Rostock vol. 66 (1982). No item-level cataloging in Kalliope.
Lower Saxony State Archives Oldenburg branch. Holdings related to Thuenen to be verified. Likely contains administrative and personal papers.

Primary personal archive at Gut Tellow (Thuenengesellschaft e.V.). Contains manuscripts, farm account books, drafts of Der isolirte Staat, personal correspondence, family papers. Thuenen lived here 1810-1850.
Mecklenburg state archive. May hold governmental records referencing Thuenen as landowner and society member.
7 items in Seedorf papers: typescripts of lectures/articles about Thuenen 1933-1959. Kalliope IDs: DE-611-HS-3163284 etc. Secondary literature manuscripts.
2 manuscripts by Edgar Salin about Thuenen (1957, 1958/1967). Kalliope IDs: CH-002121-2-991170521051405501, CH-002121-2-991170520996205501. Secondary literature.
Walter Christaller excerpt on Thuenen (pre-1931, 25 handwritten sheets). Kalliope DE-611-HS-3851963. Notable: central place theory founder studying Thuenen.
